channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Owaisi Objects to Granting Vande Mataram Same Legal Status as National Anthem
item.description: AIMIM president Asaduddin Owaisi has expressed his objection to the Union Cabinet's recent decision to give 'Vande Mataram' the same statutory protection as the national anthem 'Jana Gana Mana'. He ar...

item.title: Kenya Parliament Calls for Methane Regulations to Reduce Emissions and Protect Climate
item.description: Kenya’s Parliament is now pushing hard for laws and policies that specifically target methane emissions, which are a major factor in climate change but often ignored. The push came during a Parliament...

item.title: KDC Drives Youth Innovation and Creative Economy at Nairobi Summit
item.description: The Kenya Development Corporation (KDC) has once again demonstrated its dedication to fostering innovation and supporting young entrepreneurs as it joins the Africa Forward Summit 2026 in Nairobi from...

item.title: Court Hears Case of Man Accused of Killing Ian Watkins
item.description: A court is currently examining the case of a man accused of killing Ian Watkins, the former singer of Lostprophets who was convicted of multiple sexual offences involving minors. The trial is expected...

item.title: Coping with an Empty Nest: When Your Child Moves Out
item.description: A parent writes to advice columnist Eleanor Gordon-Smith expressing deep sadness as their son moves out, despite knowing it's a positive step for him. The parent describes feelings of grief, nostalgia...

item.title: UK Plans 45-Day Isolation for Citizens Returning from Hantavirus-Affected Cruise Ship
item.description: British authorities are preparing to implement strict isolation measures for citizens returning from the MV Hondius, a cruise ship linked to a hantavirus outbreak that has resulted in three deaths and...

item.title: Australian shares set to open lower following Wall Street dip and oil price decline
item.description: Australian shares are expected to open lower after Wall Street retreated from recent record highs. Futures indicate the ASX could drop by around 137 points, or 1.5 per cent, following a 1 per cent gai...

item.title: BJP serves defamation notice to Punjab CM after allegations linking party to Jalandhar and Amritsar blasts
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has issued a defamation notice to Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann following his remarks linking the party to two low-intensity explosions that occurred in Jalandha...

item.title: Two-Wheeler Riders and Pedestrians Account for Majority of Road Deaths in India, 2024
item.description: According to the latest National Crime Records Bureau (NCRB) report, road accidents in India claimed over 1.1 lakh lives of two-wheeler riders and pedestrians in 2024, constituting 64% of the total 1....

item.title: First Past the Post Undermines Democratic Representation in UK Elections
item.description: Polly Toynbee argues that the UK's first-past-the-post (FPTP) electoral system continues to distort democratic representation. With multiple parties now competing closely, FPTP often grants disproport...

item.title: Vehicle targets Jewish schoolboys in North London in a religiously motivated incident
item.description: In North London, a disturbing incident occurred in which a car was driven towards a group of boys attending a Jewish school. Authorities have confirmed that the attack is being treated as a 'religious...

item.title: Investigation Underway Into Hantavirus Cases on Cruise Ship
item.description: Health authorities from Cabo Verde, the Netherlands, Spain, South Africa and the UK are coordinating an international response to hantavirus cases linked to the MV Hondius cruise ship. As of 6 May 202...

item.title: Director John Irvin reflects on his 1991 anti-Thatcher Robin Hood film and rivalry with Kevin Costner’s blockbuster
item.description: Thirty-five years ago, two very different films about the legendary outlaw Robin Hood were released in close succession. While Kevin Costner’s Hollywood blockbuster Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves domin...

item.title: Ted Turner’s Lasting Impact on Global Television and News
item.description: Ted Turner, who passed away on April 6, 2026, was a pioneering figure in the world of television, best known for founding CNN, the first 24-hour news network. His vision transformed how people access ...

item.title: KDC Supports Youth-Led Innovation and Creative Economy at Africa Forward Summit 2026
item.description: Kenya Development Corporation (KDC) has reaffirmed its commitment to backing youth-led innovation and the creative economy as it gears up for the Africa Forward Summit 2026, happening on May 11–12 in ...

item.title: Government and Parents Work to Protect Children from Risks on Roblox
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – Philippine authorities, led by the Cybercrime Investigation and Coordinating Center (CICC) and the Council for the Welfare of Children (CWC), are urging stronger safeguards on Ro...

item.title: Federal High Court to Decide on Admissibility of EFCC Witness Extra-Judicial Statement in Yahaya Bello Money Laundering Trial
item.description: Justice Emeka Nwite of the Federal High Court for Abuja don fix June 15 to give ruling on whether one extra-judicial statement by EFCC witness fit enter as evidence under the Evidence Act for the ongo...

item.title: CHED Rolls Out Reframed General Education Curriculum Despite Concerns
item.description: The article discusses the recent rollout of the Reframed General Education Curriculum by the Commission on Higher Education (CHED) in the Philippines. The new curriculum reduces the previous nine core...

item.title: Jennifer Aniston Filming Season Five of The Morning Show in Los Angeles
item.description: Jennifer Aniston was seen on set filming the fifth season of Apple TV+'s The Morning Show in downtown Los Angeles. At 56, she displayed a youthful appearance, wearing a tan coat and sweater complement...

item.title: DOE Files Complaint Against Leandro Leviste and SPBC for Failing to Deliver Solar Energy Services
item.description: The Department of Energy (DOE) has filed a legal complaint with the Department of Justice against Batangas Representative Leandro Leviste and five other officials of the Solar Para sa Bayan Corporatio...
